title: androcalva inglewoodensis
intro: Androcalva inglewoodensis, also known as the Tasmanian peppermint bush, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains menthone and limonene, making it useful for treating digestive issues, nausea, and fever. The health benefits of Androcalva inglewoodensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and fighting off infections. Androcalva inglewoodensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or infused oil, offering various options for its utilization in traditional medicine.
